Bacterial leaf blight.cause: Bacterial infection spread through water and infected plant debris
Bacterial leaf blight.treatment: Apply a copper-based fungicide to control the spread of the disease
Bacterial leaf blight.prevention: Avoid overhead irrigation to minimize leaf wetness. Plant resistant varieties if available

Brown spot.cause: Fungal infection that thrives in warm, humid conditions
Brown spot.treatment: Use a fungicide containing propiconazole or azoxystrobin. Improve field drainage and ensure balanced nutrition
Brown spot.prevention: Ensure proper spacing between plants to improve air circulation. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization

Leaf smut.cause: Fungal spores present in soil and on plant debris
Leaf smut.treatment: Remove and destroy infected plant parts. Apply a fungicide containing mancozeb or carbendazim
Leaf smut.prevention: Practice crop rotation to reduce the buildup of the pathogen in the soil. Use certified disease-free seeds

Healthy.cause: Plant shows no signs of disease
Healthy.prevention: Maintain regular disease monitoring and good farming practices
